notes: Ruhamah = "Having obtained mercy," or "those who have obtained mercy"
mercy (Heb. racham) to love, love deeply, have mercy, be compassionate, have tender affection, have compassion
whoredoms (Heb. zanuwn) adultery, fornication, prostitution from zanah "play the harlot"
lovers = The priests, priestesses, and idols of the Canaanite temples or, in the larger sense, any person

quotes.text: The image of prostitution was commonly used in the Old Testament to depict extreme apostasy not only from religion but from all that is godly and good (Isa. 1:21; 23:16; Jer. 2:20-31; 13:27; Ezek. 16:15, 28-29; Hosea 2:5; Nahum 3:4).
quotes.attr: Donald W. Parry and Jay A. Parry, Understanding the Book of Revelation, p.219

notes: wall (Heb. gader) fence or wall from the root gadar "to wall up, wall off, close off"
path (Heb. nathiyb) pathway from from an unused root meaning to tramp
thorns (Heb. ciyr) caldron from a primitive root meaning to boil up
overtake (Heb. nasag) to reach, overtake, take hold upon
lewdness (Heb. nabluwth) immodesty, shamelessness from nabal "foolishness"
deliver (Heb. natsal) to snatch away, deliver, rescue, save

quotes.text: The Lord further explained to Hosea concerning the millennial state of animals: "And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven, and with the creeping things of the ground: and I will break the bow and the sword and the battle out of the earth, and will make them to lie down safely." (Hosea 2:18.)
quotes.speaker: Gerald E. Jones
quotes.attr: Gerald E. Jones, "The Gospel and Animals," Ensign, Aug. 1972, 62

quotes.text: As in the beginning, the whole face of the earth will be a delightful garden, with the major land surfaces joined together instead of separated by oceans. (See D&C 133:23-24.) The transfiguration of the earth will be so radical and complete, that it will be "a new earth." (Isa. 65:17.) Animals of the earth will live together in harmony as the natural cycle of predator and prey will be broken: "In that day the enmity . . . of beasts, yea, the enmity of all flesh, shall cease from before my face." (D&C 101:26.) Instead of animals eating animals, they will eat straw and other natural by-products of plant life. (See Isa. 11:6-7; 65:25; Hosea 2:18.) Thus, a harmonious relationship will exist on earth between nature, animals, and humans.
quotes.speaker: Victor L. Ludlow
quotes.source: Principles and Practices of the Restored Gospel
quotes.attr: Victor L. Ludlow, Principles and Practices of the Restored Gospel, p.629

quotes.text: Importantly, Hosea was the first of the Old Testament prophets to correlate the covenant of marriage and the Lord's covenant with Israel, a characteristic whose implications have been spelled out by Abraham Heschel. Harlotry, for the Lord, expressed the antithesis of his covenant. In his anger, he asserted that "the spirit of whoredoms hath caused [Israel] to err, and they have gone a whoring from under their God. . . . Therefore your daughters shall commit who-redom, and your spouses shall commit adultery" (Hosea 4:12-13). While the imagery is not enticing, the point is abundantly clear: straying from the covenant of the Lord is tantamount to abandoning one's marriage covenant, deserting one's spouse for a harlot. One additional component is formed by the concept that coming to "know the Lord" is analogous to coming to know one's spouse in a union that is born in a covenant and proven over time with fidelity, love, and mutual trust. Once again, harlotry constituted the opposite.
quotes.attr: S. Kent Brown in Kent P. Jackson, ed., Studies in Scripture, Vol. 4: 1 Kings to Malachi [Salt Lake City: Deseret Book Co., 1993], 65
